title: DI Decision Signal Payload
description: Signal emitted by Dev.IQ skills after a DI four-layer assessment. Consumed by the telemetry sink configured in .dev-iq/telemetry-overlay.md.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for this assessment run (e.g. UUID or CI job ID).

commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA at the time of assessment.

pr_id.description: PR / work item identifier if this assessment is PR-scoped, otherwise null.

maturity_tier.description: Maturity tier from .dev-iq/maturity-profile.md at the time of assessment. Drives verdict autonomy.

signal_type.description: Discriminator for which Dev.IQ skill produced this signal.

layers.description: Per-layer state and evidence collected during the DI assessment.

intent.description: INTENT — are we building the right thing? Work item coverage and AC alignment.

work_item_id.description: Linked work item ID (ADO, Jira, GitHub Issue).

ac_count.description: Number of acceptance criteria defined on the work item.

ac_covered.description: Number of ACs addressed by this change.

design.description: DESIGN — are we building it the right way? Architecture, patterns, naming, abstractions.

lint_score.description: Lint score from configured lint tool (0–100 scale if available).

pattern_violations.description: Count of design findings (naming, layer separation, abstraction).

breaking_api_changes.description: True if public API or interface contract was modified.

quality.description: QUALITY — is it production-ready? Coverage, SAST, error handling.

coverage_pct.description: Line/branch coverage percentage from the CI coverage report.

risk.description: RISK — what could break? Blast radius, breaking changes, dependency health.

services_touched.description: Logical services or modules touched by this change.

schema_changes.description: True if a database migration or schema change is included.

decision_confidence.description: Synthesized verdict — the combined answer to 'is this safe to proceed?' No single metric substitutes for all four layers.

verdict.description: Go = all layers STRONG or WEAK with no blockers. Hold = Critical/High security finding or UNGRADED layer. Discuss = findings present that require human review before proceeding.

ungraded_layers.description: Layers that could not be assessed due to missing tooling or data.

blocking_findings.description: Human-readable descriptions of findings that block the verdict (Critical/High security, UNGRADED layer).
